steps.title: Expand both sides
steps.content: Distribute on each side of the equation:$$6(x+15)+4x=6x+90+4x=10x+90$$$$10(x+9)=10x+90$$

steps.title: Compare the expressions
steps.content: The equation becomes $$10x+90=10x+90$$Subtracting the same expression from both sides gives $0=0$, which is true for every real value of $x$.

steps.title: Count the solutions
steps.content: Since every real number satisfies the original equation, there is no single restricted value and no contradiction. The equation has infinitely many solutions. The answer is C.
